title: Canada's Leader Says Friendship with U.S. is Now a Problem
summary: The Prime Minister of Canada, Mark Carney, said that Canada's close economic friendship with the United States used to be a good thing but is now a weakness. He explained this in a video message. Carney talked about how the U.S. has changed its trade rules by increasing taxes on goods, which hurts Canadian workers in car and steel industries. He said this makes businesses unsure about investing money. Carney wants to fix this by making new trade deals with other countries and giving updates to Canadians. He also mentioned that the world is more dangerous and divided now. Carney has experience as a bank leader and has spoken about these issues before at big meetings.

background_read: Canada and the United States have been close friends and trading partners for a long time, sharing a big border and many businesses. Trade means buying and selling goods between countries, which helps economies grow. Recently, the U.S. has made changes to trade rules, like increasing tariffs, which are taxes on imported goods. This can make it harder for Canadian companies to sell their products in the U.S. and can affect jobs. Leaders like Prime Minister Carney work to protect their country's economy by finding new ways to trade with other nations.
Article_Structure: WHO: Mark Carney, the Prime Minister of Canada
WHAT: He said Canada's economic ties with the U.S. are now a weakness due to trade changes
WHERE: In a video address from Vancouver, British Columbia
WHY: To explain efforts to diversify trade and address challenges from U.S. policies

perspectives.description: Carney sees the U.S. trade changes as a threat that requires action to protect Canada's economy.
